text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Banks look clean after stress tests, but the real noise is rate and war spending. An $87B Iran war bill plus hints Warsh might hike rates = bad combo for growth/tech multiples. Chip names need rate stability to hold their valuations — this threatens that.

Your holdings: Three need eyes. PLTR is down 12.77% and flagged SELL — on margin, that hole gets bigger fast; your stop at $85 is far from current price, tighten or trim now. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45%, flagged TRIM — the OpenAI custom chip news is a direct threat to Broadcom's AI revenue story. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history — tiny moves can be violent; margin makes this dangerous. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF, already down 14.48% — decay is eating you alive.

Watch: 1) Any Fed speaker confirming Warsh rate-hike talk — that's a growth stock killer. 2) AVGO's reaction to the Broadcom/OpenAI chip headline — direction tomorrow sets the tone.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk is AVGO getting hit while you're still overleveraged in a rising-rate scare.
